div1.entryFree:
Unthrifty, 1) not economical, not intent on profit: u. loveliness, why dost thou spend upon thyself thy beauty's legacy? Sonn. 4, 1. our absence makes us u. to our knowledge, Wint. V, 2, 121 (not intent on increasing, and hence not increasing, our knowledge).
2) unprofitable, unfortunate: much I fear some ill u. thing, Rom. V, 3, 136 (reading of Q2; the rest of O. Edd. unlucky).
3) good for nothing: my house left in the fearful guard of an u. knave, Merch. I, 3, 177. can no man tell me of my u. son? R2 V, 3, 1.
